description: DIAGNOSTIC: Verify APF traffic path and ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR root cause

consensus.notes: Page loads fully with sidebar, search input, Pro/Deep modes. NO ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR. Was completely JS_BLOCKED in #353 test ~25 min earlier.

claude.notes: Page loads fully with chat input, Sonnet 4.6 Extended model selector. Banner: 'We are experiencing technical difficulties'. This is CONTROL test (h2_hold=0).

diagnostic_answers.Q1_traffic_through_etap: YES — Both consensus.app and claude.ai certificates are issued by Plantynet OfficeGuarad CA_V3 (Plantynet Co., Ltd). Same CA public key fingerprint. Test PC traffic CONFIRMED going through Etap MITM bridge.
diagnostic_answers.Q2_err_http2_cause: NOT_APF_SPECIFIC — ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR from #353 was either TRANSIENT or APF config was CHANGED between tests. consensus.app (h2_hold=1) now loads perfectly through the same MITM path that caused errors 25 minutes ago.
diagnostic_answers.Q3_h2_hold_comparison: BOTH_WORK — claude.ai (h2_hold=0) and consensus.app (h2_hold=1) both load normally. h2_hold setting is NOT causing page load failures at this time. The #353 ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR was likely caused by APF actively intercepting (keyword matched or config issue) rather than h2_hold itself.

critical_finding: APF config appears to have been MODIFIED between #353 test (15:36) and #359 test (16:00). In #353, ALL h2_hold=1 services had ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on JS resources. Now consensus.app (h2_hold=1) loads perfectly. Either: (1) APF was reconfigured to not intercept page-load resources, (2) APF module was restarted/disabled, or (3) the errors were transient network issues.
recommendation: Check etap.log on Etap bridge for config changes between 15:30-16:00. If APF was intentionally fixed, re-run #353 batch test to verify all services now work. If APF was disabled, re-enable and test again to reproduce the issue.
